task_updates.executor_notes: Implemented `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/20260423000005_discord_promoter_fn.sql` with `internal.discord_promote_resources(...)` as a `SECURITY DEFINER` PL/pgSQL function over the 5 hard-coded channel IDs. The function includes the dry-run count path, write-path missing-member warning logs, anti-join `assets` and `asset_comments` inserts with `EXISTS (members)` prefilters, `discord_guild_id` copy-through, conditional `IS DISTINCT FROM` drift updates, reply resolver, media job enqueue via `regexp_match(... '/attachments/\\d+/(\\d+)/')`, attachment-removal reconciliation, comment-deletion propagation, per-channel summary logging using only `logger_name/level/message/extra`, live-path `assets.last_synced_at` refresh, and an `EXCEPTION` logger with `SQLERRM` + `SQLSTATE`. Verification: the migration file parsed successfully via `psql -f` on scratch PostgreSQL 17 after creating `internal` and `pgcrypto`; the pre-existing `supabase db reset` blocker from T3 remains unchanged upstream, so the full repo migration chain still cannot be applied end-to-end yet. One intentional deviation: the returned dry-run flag column is named `dry_run_result`, because PostgreSQL rejects the exact `dry_run` input/output name duplication in PL/pgSQL.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Mostly yes. The implemented function is `SECURITY DEFINER`, keeps the dry-run count path separate from live writes, uses `WHERE NOT EXISTS` + `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` on the asset/comment inserts, keeps the `EXISTS (members)` prefilter, copies `m.guild_id` into both assets and comments, uses the channel→type CASE, enqueues media jobs from `regexp_match(...)`, reconciles removed attachments, propagates `discord_messages.is_deleted`, and all log writes use only `logger_name/level/message/extra`. The one deliberate deviation is the returned flag column name: PostgreSQL rejects the exact `dry_run` input/output name duplication in PL/pgSQL, so the function returns `dry_run_result` instead. The migration itself parsed successfully on scratch PostgreSQL 17.

Yes. All four resource hooks now select `source`, `discord_guild_id`, `discord_channel_id`, `discord_thread_id`, and `is_hidden`, and each asset query adds `.eq('is_hidden', false)`. In this repo, `useResources` lives under `src/pages/Resources/useResources.ts`, so that actual file was updated rather than the stale plan path. The website still type-checks/builds via `npm run build`, and `npm test -- --run` passes. `supabase gen types` was rerun, but the linked-project output still lacks the new Discord-sync schema because the migrations are not deployed remotely yet.
